patch.value: Calendar view/format did-you-mean + dependency and type-safety cleanup

Agent-UX + hygiene. (1) Calendar parseView/parseOutputFormat now emit a Levenshtein 'Did you mean' suggestion on a mistyped --view/--format instead of just listing choices (never-block UX; reuses core levenshteinDistanceWithinLimit). (2) Removed unused devDependency c8 (coverage runs via @vitest/coverage-v8; c8 had no script/config reference) — drops 321 transitive lock lines. (3) Wired the previously-dead RiskLevel type into ItemFrontMatter.risk, replacing an inline duplicated union.
Mistyped calendar view/format suggests closest; c8 gone from lockfile; RiskLevel used
Done: calendar did-you-mean, c8 removed, RiskLevel used.

Calendar suggestClosestChoice did-you-mean for --view/--format (verified weeek->week, jsonn->json); removed c8 devDep + synced lockfile; RiskLevel wired into ItemFrontMatter.risk. Full suite 2012 tests green; static gate green.
